name: Multi-Agent Market Intelligence Orchestrator
description: Coordinator agent spawns 4 parallel research sub-agents on different topic angles, collects JSON results, synthesizes a final intelligence report
takeaway: Fan-out/fan-in pattern works cleanly in OpenClaw. Key gotcha: sub-agents must use exec+heredoc for file writes (not write tool) at depth 1/2. Parallel spawn cut research time from ~280s to ~70s.

name: Alert Brain — AI-Powered Alert Dispatcher
description: Flask webhook server that routes any JSON event through Claude for triage (severity, summary, channel routing), persists to JSON store, and dispatches rich embeds to Discord with per-severity channel routing and a live dashboard
takeaway: AI-as-middleware is the key pattern: LLM sits between ingestion and action, turning unstructured events into structured routing decisions. Dual AI/rule fallback makes the pipeline resilient. The prompt-for-JSON technique is highly teachable.

name: Pulse — AI Daily Market Briefing Pipeline
description: Cron-triggered pipeline that fetches Yahoo Finance prices + RSS news, synthesizes a market briefing with Claude (template fallback if no key), saves timestamped markdown reports, and delivers rich Discord embeds. Includes dark-mode history dashboard and drop-in openclaw-cron.yaml for instant scheduling.
takeaway: AI-as-synthesizer is the reusable pattern: data → LLM → formatted output. The template fallback proves the pipeline is LLM-optional, which is a first-class design principle. State persistence via run_log.json transforms a script into a pipeline. The dashboard + cron config makes this a client-shippable product, not just a code exercise.

name: Sage — Autonomous Web Research Agent
description: End-to-end research pipeline: LLM decomposes any topic into sub-questions, parallel search (Brave API / DuckDuckGo fallback), parallel content extraction (requests + BeautifulSoup), cross-source Claude synthesis (executive summary, key findings, Q&A, consensus/conflict analysis), JSON + Markdown output, Flask dashboard to browse reports
takeaway: Query decomposition is the research quality multiplier — searching N focused sub-questions beats one broad query every time. The fan-out (parallel search/fetch) + serial synthesis pattern is the correct architecture for I/O-bound agent work. Graceful degradation (LLM → template, Brave → DDG) is non-negotiable for demoable portfolio pieces. The openclaw_demo.py bridge file is a novel tutorial asset — mapping Python pipeline steps to OpenClaw tool calls teaches the framework without needing a live agent.

name: finsnap — Financial Snapshot OpenClaw Skill
description: Complete ClawHub-ready skill package: SKILL.md manifest teaches the agent to fetch Yahoo Finance price/metrics + news (no API key), synthesize AI bull/bear analysis, and deliver rich Discord embeds. Backed by bash fetch scripts, Python orchestrator, clawhub.json publishing manifest, auto-installer, and demo output.
takeaway: The SKILL.md is not documentation — it is the product. In OpenClaw, the agent instruction layer IS the primary engineering surface; the scripts are implementation detail. The full skill lifecycle (SKILL.md → scripts → clawhub.json → install.sh) is the differentiator that separates skill authors from people who just write Python scripts. Graceful degradation (Anthropic → OpenAI → template; webhook → inline text) is the professional standard. Finance domain encoding in the synthesis prompt (52wk range framing, bull/bear structure, one-word sentiment) shows LLM prompt design maturity.

name: Iris — AI Portfolio Intelligence Dashboard
description: Flask REST API backend + dark-mode HTML/JS dashboard showing live portfolio prices (Yahoo Finance), per-position LLM analyst commentary (Claude → template fallback), real-time agent activity feed, and an OpenClaw agent integration layer demonstrating push (Discord embed) and pull (refresh + poll) patterns.
takeaway: The dashboard is not the product — the API is. A client-facing dashboard in an OpenClaw deployment is the visible layer of an agent pipeline; the REST API (/api/portfolio, /api/refresh/<T>) is what makes it agent-native. Designing the API for agent consumption first (structured JSON, cache invalidation endpoint, agent activity log) is the pattern that separates a demo from a production integration.

name: Mnemosyne — Persistent Memory & Context Injection Agent
description: SQLite-backed memory store with TF-IDF semantic search (no API needed), importance + recency scoring, full context injection pipeline, Flask REST API, dark-mode dashboard with search/chat/add tabs, and an OpenClaw tool mapping bridge for teaching. 18 realistic seed memories. Template fallback active without API key.
takeaway: The context block is the product. The memory store is plumbing, the semantic search is routing — what matters is the formatted [MEMORY CONTEXT] block that lands in the LLM prompt. TF-IDF from scratch (no sklearn) works well for factual memories and signals math comfort. The injected_prompt field in the API response is the killer teaching feature: it makes the invisible visible. The openclaw_agent.py bridge (Python function → OpenClaw tool call mapping) is the pattern that should appear in every Forge build going forward.
